headline: Vaud prepares for packed June 14 ballot as voting materials arrive
summary: — Vaud electors are receiving ballot materials between 18 and 22 May for the 14 June vote, which bundles the federal SVP population-cap initiative with two cantonal measures: a constitutional minimum-wage guarantee, and a requirement that subsidised housing projects split units equally between owner

headline: SVP "No to 10 Million" enters final fortnight — near-tied in polls, party vicepresident recruits EU workers
summary: — The 14 June federal popular initiative to cap Switzerland's resident population at 10 million by 2050 is polling near-tied (48% in favour, 45% opposed, December 2025 surveys).

headline: Bilaterals III: Parliament debates dispatch, popular vote target 2027
summary: — The Federal Council's dispatch on the Bilaterals III package (signed 2 March 2026) has been before both chambers of the Federal Parliament since 13 March.

headline: NCSC H2 2025 report: 64,733 incident reports, AI-aided targeting now standard
summary: — The National Cyber Security Centre's second-half 2025 report (published 2026) records 64,733 voluntary reports for the full year — roughly 2,000 more than 2024 — while the headline finding is qualitative: threat actors are increasingly using artificial intelligence to tailor their attacks, shiftin

headline: G7 Evian (June 15–17): Geneva, Lausanne, and Lake Geneva on high-security alert
summary: — The G7 summit at Evian-les-Bains, France — 45 km from Geneva — will impose significant security restrictions on the entire Lake Geneva region.

headline: Rebecca Ruiz departs Vaud State Council after seven years
summary: — The Vaud Grand Council formally bid farewell on 19 May to Rebecca Ruiz, head of the Department of Health and Social Action since 2019.

headline: Russia deploys nuclear warheads to Belarus in largest drills in years — NATO responds with simulated strike
summary: — Russia delivered Iskander-M-capable nuclear munitions to field storage sites in Belarus as part of exercises mobilising 64,000 personnel, framed by Moscow as a response to Western support for Ukraine and drone incursions over the Baltic states.

headline: Iran-US: ceasefire on "life support" as dual blockade of Hormuz persists into May 22
summary: — Iran said Thursday it has not yet accepted the latest US peace proposal and continues to frame the American naval blockade (in force since 13 April, targeting ships bound for Iranian ports) as a ceasefire violation.

headline: DOJ indicts Raúl Castro on murder and conspiracy charges; military intervention option raised
summary: — The US Department of Justice filed criminal charges against former Cuban president Raúl Castro and five Cuban air-force officers on 20 May 2026, arising from the 1996 shoot-down of two civilian Brothers to the Rescue aircraft over international waters that killed four US nationals.

headline: Trump-Xi Beijing summit: "strategic stability" framework, limited concrete deliverables
summary: — The 14–15 May summit produced a guiding framework of "constructive China-US strategic stability" and two headline business deals: China committed to order 200 Boeing jets (short of the 500 initially floated) and the US approved Nvidia H200 chip sales to major Chinese companies.

headline: Bolivia: Paz government faces capital siege, deepening political crisis
summary: — President Rodrigo Paz faces widespread protests and road blockades with the political capital La Paz effectively under siege less than six months after he took office following 2025's contested elections.

headline: UN expresses "grave concern" over Taliban child-marriage law
summary: — The United Nations expressed grave concern over a new Taliban government ordinance on marriage separation that includes provisions permitting child marriage, calling it a further rollback of fundamental rights for Afghan women and girls.

headline: CVE-2026-41091 & CVE-2026-45498 — Microsoft Defender zero-days actively exploited; CISA KEV 20 May
summary: — Two Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities confirmed exploited in the wild.

headline: CVE-2025-34291 & CVE-2026-34926 — Langflow RCE and Trend Micro Apex One traversal; CISA KEV 21 May
summary: — **CVE-2025-34291** (CVSS 9.4, Langflow origin-validation error): three weaknesses in combination — overly permissive CORS, absent CSRF protection, and a code-execution endpoint exposed by design — allow an attacker to gain full remote code execution on the Langflow server and exfiltrate every API

headline: CVE-2026-20223 — Cisco Secure Workload CVSS 10.0: unauthenticated cross-tenant API access
summary: — Cisco disclosed this maximum-severity flaw on 21–22 May.

headline: TanStack/TeamPCP supply chain worm: 170+ npm packages, first SLSA-provenance bypass, Grafana source exposed
summary: — On 11 May 2026 (19:20–19:26 UTC), threat group TeamPCP hijacked TanStack's own OIDC-backed CI pipeline to inject malicious artifacts into 42 `@tanstack` npm packages (84 total artifacts), exploiting a trusted package-publish token that the pipeline generated and which an attacker-controlled cache

headline: MuddyWater (Seedworm) Iranian APT poses as Chaos ransomware in corporate espionage campaign
summary: — A Broadcom/SecurityWeek analysis of an early-2026 intrusion attributes the campaign to MuddyWater (Seedworm / Static Kitten / Mango Sandstorm), affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS).

headline: arXiv:2605.22438
summary: (submitted ~21 May 2026, announced 22 May) — *Do Not Trust The Auctioneer: Learning to Bid in Feedback-Manipulated Auctions* — An RL and online-learning result for repeated first-price auctions in which the auctioneer plants shill bids that inflate the observed losing bid without changing actual all

headline: arXiv:2605.22549
summary: (submitted 21 May 2026, announced 22 May) — *A Martingale Kernel Independence Test* — Proposes studentised HSIC statistics whose null distribution is asymptotically standard normal under independence, eliminating the permutation-calibration step that makes HSIC calibration O(B × n²) per test in prac

headline: arXiv:2605.22795
summary: (submitted ~21–22 May 2026, announced 22 May) — *Finite-Particle Convergence Rates for Conservative and Non-Conservative Drifting Models* — Analyses one-step generative models ("drifting models") in which a source distribution is transported to a target.
